title: Lecture Three Parity-Anomaly Half-Level Context
summary: This equation context records Witten's continuum derivation that one massive 2+1-dimensional Dirac fermion induces a half-level Chern-Simons term, while a doubled pair with opposite masses restores an integer-gauge-invariant total.
assumptions: The massive Dirac fermion can be integrated out at low energies.
The normalized momentum-space map controls the induced Chern-Simons term.
scope: Equation-level context for the parity-anomaly part of the Lecture Three theorem family.

source_anchors.notes: Witten's continuum derivation of the half-level and its doubling cancellation.

representation: Equation context for the half-level parity anomaly and its cancellation by doubling.

math_expressions.notes: Effective Chern-Simons term induced by one massive Dirac fermion.
Massive Dirac Hamiltonian used in the Berry-flux argument.
Momentum-space map to a hemisphere, giving half a degree.
Two opposite masses cancel the half-level anomaly.

symbols.meaning: Dirac mass whose sign determines the induced half level

retrieval_hints: Open this when an answer must explain the half-level carefully rather than only invoking 'parity anomaly'.
